title: Resolved workaround
body:
... per https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1558 .

Hopefully fixes #1567, but absolutely untested.

comments.body:
@cevich would it be easy enough for you to test this in the broken environment again?

Would it work for this PR to (temporarily/permanently?) change the `contrib/cirrus/runner.sh` update of `nsswitch.conf`?

> would it be easy enough for you to test this in the broken environment again?

Oh ya, you can do that right in this PR.  Just exactly as you suggest, yoink-out the `nsswitch.conf` update in `contrib/cirrus/runner.sh`.  That will put the VM back into the odd but stock (at least for GCE) environment.  You'll know in a few minutes if the tests fail or not.

`resolv.conf` managed by google-networking ***not*** systemd-resolved
`nsswitch.conf` including lookup to systemd-resolved

***Note:*** I'm also not above needing a fix done at the VM-build level, it's possible our setup is simply wrong and bad as-is.  After all, I'm kind of just blindly throwing darts in the general direction of the target and hoping some stick.

Oof...not a very helpful error message.  I have no idea what that means, but it does seem the tests got further along.
```
...cut...
Output registry-5006 stderr: got 176,<nil>: time="2022-02-11T18:30:28.336868497-06:00" level=info msg="listening on :5006" go.version=go1.16.13 instance.id=c10fcd9f-184d-44f3-8a1d-b8f59c48e820 openshift.logger=registry 
Output registry-5006 stderr: waiting
Sleeping for port 5006 done, will retry
Checking for port 5006...
Port 5006 open
Port checker for port 5006 exiting
OK, Registry port open
oc login
Running oc new-project myns
copy_test.go:49:
    s.cluster = startOpenshiftCluster(c) // FIXME: Set up TLS for the docker registry port instead of using "--tls-verify=false" all over the place.
utils.go:57:
    c.Assert(err, check.IsNil, check.Commentf("%s", out))
... value *exec.ExitError = &exec.ExitError{ProcessState:(*os.ProcessState)(0xc00000e108), Stderr:[]uint8(nil)} ("exit status 1")
... Unable to connect to the server: stream error: stream ID 7; INTERNAL_ERROR
```

Apparently “stream error” has something to do with HTTP 2. Who would have guessed.

Anyway, I’m sure I should take the time to understand the reliability and all the complaints of that historic version of OpenShift but it’s not happening this week. I’ll just hit the re-run button.

> I’ll just hit the re-run button.

Oh okay, you think it's just a transitory error (i.e. Flake).  I have a hard time telling based on the old OS output :confused:

Further progress into yet another WTF failure, [system-tests this time](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4652101262376960?logs=system#L26):

```
...cut...
not ok 2 inspect: basic
# (from function `expect_output' in file systemtest/helpers.bash, line 213,
#  in test file systemtest/010-inspect.bats, line 33)
#   `expect_output --from="$inspect_local" --substring "sha256:$sha" \' failed
# $ /usr/bin/skopeo inspect docker://quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest
...cut...
```
Ignoring the "how did this pass before" question...maybe the test command is just missing a `--format`?

> Oh okay, you think it's just a transitory error (i.e. Flake). I have a hard time telling based on the old OS output 😕

Those were failures to start OpenShift, weren’t they? OpenShift is test infrastructure, not the subject of the test. (Sure, a flake in either costs us the same avoidable work. But there’s less risk of a product bug when the environment doesn’t come up.)

> Ignoring the "how did this pass before" question...

A hypothesis in email — AFAICS the image used in the test changed recently. Maybe #1572 .
Tests pass in https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5504393163309056 https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6630293070151680 , so I’ll re-introduce the resolved workaround again.
